reset_tests.description: At power-up (no explicit reset) drive bin=0 and verify gray=0 immediately, confirming default combinational mapping before any other stimulus.

normal_tests.description: Basic incremental sweep across low values to ensure bin^(bin>>1) relationship; exercise representative points without full enumeration.

corner_case_tests.description: Boundary extremes ensure MSB handling and XOR ripple at edges.
Alternating-bit patterns stress every XOR pair for independence.
Single-bit walks detect bit-to-bit crosstalk and ensure each XOR pair only depends on adjacent bits.

sequence_tests.description: Glitch-hunting transition test: apply successive bin values differing in multiple bits (e.g., 0x155->0x2AA->0x3FF->0x000) and sample gray immediately to confirm purely combinational response without meta-stable lag.
Walking Gray verification: iterate bin from 0 to 1023 sequentially (can be accelerated via for-loop) and compare gray trajectory to expected Gray code; ensures no history dependence or stale outputs.

random_tests.description: Random 10-bit bin values with on-the-fly computation of bin^(bin>>1); capture mismatches to catch mutants with localized bit mistakes.

key_discriminators: Every gray[i] must equal bin[i+1]^bin[i]; any mutant miswiring (e.g., using OR or wrong neighbor) is exposed by alternating-bit and single-bit-walk vectors.
MSB gray[9] must mirror bin[9]; tests that toggle only bin[9] will isolate mutants that continue XORing with nonexistent bits.
Sequence transitions confirm outputs update combinationally without clock/latched behavior; any sequential mutant exhibiting holdover will fail during rapid value sweeps.
Exhaustive sweep (feasible for 10 bits) ensures no mutant survives due to untested bin combinations.
